success_surface: Robust date parsing with multi-format support combined with reference data extraction and validation before write-back
effective_behaviors: Comprehensive date format detection and parsing with fallback strategies
Building in-memory lookup dictionaries from reference data before processing
Exploring sheet structure programmatically before implementing logic
Using openpyxl for formatting preservation while processing cell values
Iterative verification of sample outputs before final save
Graceful error handling with informative warnings for skipped rows
evidence: Agent tried multiple datetime parsing formats ('%Y-%m-%d', '%Y/%m/%d', '%d/%m/%Y', '%m/%d/%Y') to handle inconsistent date cells
Extracted 15 holidays into a dictionary before iterating through 74 target rows for efficient O(1) lookups
Printed sheet structure info (max_row, max_column, header values) before implementing holiday extraction logic
Used load_workbook() instead of pandas-read_excel to preserve non-data elements like formatting
Verified first 10 processed rows showing correct day-of-week and holiday values before saving file
Implemented try/except blocks around date parsing with warning logs for rows where parsing failed

memory_items.title: Multi-Format Date Parsing
memory_items.lesson: Excel cells may contain dates in various formats (datetime objects, strings, timestamps). Always attempt multiple parsing strategies sequentially.
memory_items.generalization: When reading date columns, check if value is already a datetime object first. If string, try common formats in order. Support pandas Timestamp types if applicable.

memory_items.title: Reference Data Extraction Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When processing requires lookup against external criteria (holidays, categories, mappings), extract reference data into an in-memory structure first.
memory_items.generalization: Identify all reference rows/columns that define mapping rules, build a dictionary/map structure, then iterate through target data using the lookup for efficiency.

memory_items.title: Pre-Processing Sheet Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Always discover sheet structure (row/column counts, headers, data locations) before writing processing code.
memory_items.generalization: Query wb.sheetnames, ws.max_row, ws.max_column, and sample header values to confirm assumptions about data layout.

memory_items.title: Verification Before Final Save
memory_items.lesson: Print sample processed outputs to verify correctness before saving the workbook permanently.
memory_items.generalization: After processing but before wb.save(), print a few rows showing key input/output pairs to catch logic errors early.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Date Format Robustness**: When reading date cells, implement a cascading parse strategy that checks: (1) native datetime type, (2) pandas Timestamp, (3) multiple string formats. Never assume a single format.
- **Lookup Data Preparation**: For tasks requiring conditional logic based on reference data (holidays, categories, mappings), scan and build a dictionary/map structure before processing the main dataset for O(1) access.
- **Pre-Implementation Discovery**: Query sheet dimensions, headers, and sample content values before writing transformation logic. Print these findings to validate assumptions.
- **Intermediate Verification**: After processing batches of data, print a representative sample of inputs and outputs to verify correctness before committing changes via save().
